title: T-068 — Docs: TEC-052 Cost Import from D365 prototype gap brief
prompt:
# T-068 — Docs: TEC-052 Cost Import from D365 prototype gap brief

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §11.5 TEC-052, §4A TEC-052
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Brief unblocking TEC-052 prototype design.

Document diff preview row schema (current cost, incoming cost, delta, source), the batch-confirm guardrails (V-TEC-53 high-variance approver), and audit note format.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- design/specs/TEC-052-cost-import-d365.md [create]

## Out of scope
- Prototype JSX
- Implementation

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the brief, when reviewed, then diff preview schema, V-TEC-53 enforcement, and audit-note flow are specified.
2. Given the brief, when read by a designer, then a labelled prototype can be produced.

## Test strategy
- Manual review.
- No automated tests for docs.

## Risk red lines
- Do not require live D365 access for this doc.
- D365 work must be optional integration/export/import from Monopilot-owned data; no strategic dependency or silent canonical overwrite.
- Do not claim the screen is blocked by missing prototype; implementation is covered by the paired spec-driven UI task.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
